hook.text: You breathe in oxygen and breathe out carbon dioxide. Inside your cells, the real story unfolds.

buildup.text: Glucose and oxygen enter the mitochondria. A chain of reactions breaks glucose apart.

discovery.text: The process yields up to 36 ATP molecules from a single glucose molecule.

twist.text: Most ATP comes from the electron transport chain, not from breaking glucose directly.

climax.text: Without oxygen, cells switch to fermentation and produce far less energy.

punchline.text: Breathing feeds your cells, not just your lungs.

quiz.question: How many ATP molecules can one glucose molecule yield?
